verses.latin_vulgate: Quoniam quidem multi conati sunt ordinare narrationem, quæ in nobis completæ sunt, rerum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐπειδήπερ πολλοὶ ἐπεχείρησαν ἀνατάξασθαι διήγησιν περὶ τῶν πεπληροφορημένων ἐν ἡμῖν πραγμάτων
verses.king_james: Forasmuch as many have taken in hand to set forth in order a declaration of those things which are most surely believed among us,

verses.latin_vulgate: sicut tradiderunt nobis, qui ab initio ipsi viderunt, et ministri fuerunt sermonis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καθὼς παρέδοσαν ἡμῖν οἱ ἀπ᾽ ἀρχῆς αὐτόπται καὶ ὑπηρέται γενόμενοι τοῦ λόγου
verses.king_james: Even as they delivered them unto us, which from the beginning were eyewitnesses, and ministers of the word;

verses.latin_vulgate: visum est et mihi, assecuto omnia a principio diligenter, ex ordine tibi scribere, optime Theophile,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔδοξεν κἀμοὶ παρηκολουθηκότι ἄνωθεν πᾶσιν ἀκριβῶς καθεξῆς σοι γράψαι κράτιστε φεόφιλε
verses.king_james: It seemed good to me also, having had perfect understanding of all things from the very first, to write unto thee in order, most excellent Theophilus,

verses.latin_vulgate: ut cognoscas eorum verborum, de quibus eruditus es, veritatem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἵνα ἐπιγνῷς περὶ ὧν κατηχήθης λόγων τὴν ἀσφάλειαν
verses.king_james: That thou mightest know the certainty of those things, wherein thou hast been instructed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fuit in diebus Herodis, regis Judææ, sacerdos quidam nomine Zacharias de vice Abia, et uxor illius de filiabus Aaron, et nomen ejus Elisabeth
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγένετο ἐν ταῖς ἡμέραις ἡρῴδου τοῦ βασιλέως τῆς ἰουδαίας ἱερεύς τις ὀνόματι ξαχαρίας ἐξ ἐφημερίας ἀβιά καὶ ἡ γυνὴ αὐτοῦ ἐκ τῶν θυγατέρων ἀαρών καὶ τὸ ὄνομα αὐτῆς ἐλισάβετ
verses.king_james: There was in the days of Herod, the king of Judaea, a certain priest named Zacharias, of the course of Abia: and his wife *was* of the daughters of Aaron, and her name *was* Elisabeth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erant autem justi ambo ante Deum, incedentes in omnibus mandatis et justificationibus Domini sine querela.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦσαν δὲ δίκαιοι ἀμφότεροι ἐνώπιον τοῦ θεοῦ πορευόμενοι ἐν πάσαις ταῖς ἐντολαῖς καὶ δικαιώμασιν τοῦ κυρίου ἄμεμπτοι
verses.king_james: And they were both righteous before God, walking in all the commandments and ordinances of the Lord blameless.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et non erat illis filius, eo quod esset Elisabeth sterilis, et ambo processissent in diebus suis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οὐκ ἦν αὐτοῖς τέκνον καθότι ἡ ἐλισάβετ ἦν στεῖρα καὶ ἀμφότεροι προβεβηκότες ἐν ταῖς ἡμέραις αὐτῶν ἦσαν
verses.king_james: And they had no child, because that Elisabeth was barren, and they both were *now* well stricken in years.

verses.latin_vulgate: Factum est autem, cum sacerdotio fungeretur in ordine vicis suæ ante Deum,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγένετο δὲ ἐν τῷ ἱερατεύειν αὐτὸν ἐν τῇ τάξει τῆς ἐφημερίας αὐτοῦ ἔναντι τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, that while he executed the priest's office before God in the order of his course,

verses.latin_vulgate: secundum consuetudinem sacerdotii, sorte exiit ut incensum poneret, ingressus in templum Domini:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κατὰ τὸ ἔθος τῆς ἱερατείας ἔλαχεν τοῦ θυμιᾶσαι εἰσελθὼν εἰς τὸν ναὸν τοῦ κυρίου
verses.king_james: According to the custom of the priest's office, his lot was to burn incense when he went into the temple of the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: et omnis multitudo populi erat orans foris hora incensi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πᾶν τὸ πλῆθος τοῦ λαοῦ ἦν προσευχόμενον ἔξω τῇ ὥρᾳ τοῦ θυμιάματος
verses.king_james: And the whole multitude of the people were praying without at the time of incense.

verses.latin_vulgate: Apparuit autem illi angelus Domini, stans a dextris altaris incensi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὤφθη δὲ αὐτῷ ἄγγελος κυρίου ἑστὼς ἐκ δεξιῶν τοῦ θυσιαστηρίου τοῦ θυμιάματος
verses.king_james: And there appeared unto him an angel of the Lord standing on the right side of the altar of incense.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et Zacharias turbatus est videns, et timor irruit super eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐταράχθη ξαχαρίας ἰδών καὶ φόβος ἐπέπεσεν ἐπ᾽ αὐτόν
verses.king_james: And when Zacharias saw *him*, he was troubled, and fear fell upon him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ait autem ad illum angelus: Ne timeas, Zacharia, quoniam exaudita est deprecatio tua: et uxor tua Elisabeth pariet tibi filium, et vocabis nomen ejus Joannem:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν δὲ πρὸς αὐτὸν ὁ ἄγγελος μὴ φοβοῦ ξαχαρία διότι εἰσηκούσθη ἡ δέησίς σου καὶ ἡ γυνή σου ἐλισάβετ γεννήσει υἱόν σοι καὶ καλέσεις τὸ ὄνομα αὐτοῦ ἰωάννην
verses.king_james: But the angel said unto him, Fear not, Zacharias: for thy prayer is heard; and thy wife Elisabeth shall bear thee a son, and thou shalt call his name John.

verses.latin_vulgate: et erit gaudium tibi, et exsultatio, et multi in nativitate ejus gaudebunt:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἔσται χαρά σοι καὶ ἀγαλλίασις καὶ πολλοὶ ἐπὶ τῇ γεννήσει αὐτοῦ χαρήσονται
verses.king_james: And thou shalt have joy and gladness; and many shall rejoice at his birth.

verses.latin_vulgate: erit enim magnus coram Domino: et vinum et siceram non bibet, et Spiritu Sancto replebitur adhuc ex utero matris suæ:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔσται γὰρ μέγας ἐνώπιον τοῦ κυρίου καὶ οἶνον καὶ σίκερα οὐ μὴ πίῃ καὶ πνεύματος ἁγίου πλησθήσεται ἔτι ἐκ κοιλίας μητρὸς αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: For he shall be great in the sight of the Lord, and shall drink neither wine nor strong drink; and he shall be filled with the Holy Ghost, even from his mother's womb.

verses.latin_vulgate: et multos filiorum Israël convertet ad Dominum Deum ipsorum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πολλοὺς τῶν υἱῶν ἰσραὴλ ἐπιστρέψει ἐπὶ κύριον τὸν θεὸν αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: And many of the children of Israel shall he turn to the Lord their God.

verses.latin_vulgate: et ipse præcedet ante illum in spiritu et virtute Eliæ: ut convertat corda patrum in filios, et incredulos ad prudentiam justorum, parare Domino plebem perfectam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ αὐτὸς προελεύσεται ἐνώπιον αὐτοῦ ἐν πνεύματι καὶ δυνάμει ἠλίου ἐπιστρέψαι καρδίας πατέρων ἐπὶ τέκνα καὶ ἀπειθεῖς ἐν φρονήσει δικαίων ἑτοιμάσαι κυρίῳ λαὸν κατεσκευασμένον
verses.king_james: And he shall go before him in the spirit and power of Elias, to turn the hearts of the fathers to the children, and the disobedient to the wisdom of the just; to make ready a people prepared for the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixit Zacharias ad angelum: Unde hoc sciam? ego enim sum senex, et uxor mea processit in diebus suis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἶπεν ξαχαρίας πρὸς τὸν ἄγγελον κατὰ τί γνώσομαι τοῦτο ἐγὼ γάρ εἰμι πρεσβύτης καὶ ἡ γυνή μου προβεβηκυῖα ἐν ταῖς ἡμέραις αὐτῆς
verses.king_james: And Zacharias said unto the angel, Whereby shall I know this? for I am an old man, and my wife well stricken in years.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et respondens angelus dixit ei: Ego sum Gabriel, qui asto ante Deum: et missus sum loqui ad te, et hæc tibi evangelizare.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀποκριθεὶς ὁ ἄγγελος εἶπεν αὐτῷ ἐγώ εἰμι γαβριὴλ ὁ παρεστηκὼς ἐνώπιον τοῦ θεοῦ καὶ ἀπεστάλην λαλῆσαι πρὸς σὲ καὶ εὐαγγελίσασθαί σοι ταῦτα
verses.king_james: And the angel answering said unto him, I am Gabriel, that stand in the presence of God; and am sent to speak unto thee, and to shew thee these glad tidings.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ecce eris tacens, et non poteris loqui usque in diem quo hæc fiant, pro eo quod non credidisti verbis meis, quæ implebuntur in tempore suo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἰδού, ἔσῃ σιωπῶν καὶ μὴ δυνάμενος λαλῆσαι ἄχρι ἡς ἡμέρας γένηται ταῦτα ἀνθ ὧν οὐκ ἐπίστευσας τοῖς λόγοις μου οἵτινες πληρωθήσονται εἰς τὸν καιρὸν αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: And, behold, thou shalt be dumb, and not able to speak, until the day that these things shall be performed, because thou believest not my words, which shall be fulfilled in their season.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et erat plebs exspectans Zachariam: et mirabantur quod tardaret ipse in templo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἦν ὁ λαὸς προσδοκῶν τὸν ξαχαρίαν καὶ ἐθαύμαζον ἐν τῷ χρονίζειν αὐτόν ἐν τῷ ναῷ
verses.king_james: And the people waited for Zacharias, and marvelled that he tarried so long in the temple.

verses.latin_vulgate: Egressus autem non poterat loqui ad illos, et cognoverunt quod visionem vidisset in templo. Et ipse erat innuens illis, et permansit mutus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐξελθὼν δὲ οὐκ ἠδύνατο λαλῆσαι αὐτοῖς καὶ ἐπέγνωσαν ὅτι ὀπτασίαν ἑώρακεν ἐν τῷ ναῷ καὶ αὐτὸς ἦν διανεύων αὐτοῖς καὶ διέμενεν κωφός
verses.king_james: And when he came out, he could not speak unto them: and they perceived that he had seen a vision in the temple: for he beckoned unto them, and remained speechless.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et factum est, ut impleti sunt dies officii ejus, abiit in domum suam:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐγένετο ὡς ἐπλήσθησαν αἱ ἡμέραι τῆς λειτουργίας αὐτοῦ ἀπῆλθεν εἰς τὸν οἶκον αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, that, as soon as the days of his ministration were accomplished, he departed to his own house.

verses.latin_vulgate: post hos autem dies concepit Elisabeth uxor ejus, et occultabat se mensibus quinque, dicens:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μετὰ δὲ ταύτας τὰς ἡμέρας συνέλαβεν ἐλισάβετ ἡ γυνὴ αὐτοῦ καὶ περιέκρυβεν ἑαυτὴν μῆνας πέντε λέγουσα
verses.king_james: And after those days his wife Elisabeth conceived, and hid herself five months, saying,

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia sic fecit mihi Dominus in diebus, quibus respexit auferre opprobrium meum inter homines.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτι οὕτως μοι πεποίηκεν ὁ κύριος ἐν ἡμέραις αἷς ἐπεῖδεν ἀφελεῖν τὸ ὄνειδός μου ἐν ἀνθρώποις
verses.king_james: Thus hath the Lord dealt with me in the days wherein he looked on *me*, to take away my reproach among men.

verses.latin_vulgate: In mense autem sexto, missus est angelus Gabriel a Deo in civitatem Galilææ, cui nomen Nazareth,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν δὲ τῷ μηνὶ τῷ ἕκτῳ ἀπεστάλη ὁ ἄγγελος γαβριὴλ ὑπὸ τοῦ θεοῦ εἰς πόλιν τῆς γαλιλαίας ᾗ ὄνομα ναζαρὲτ
verses.king_james: And in the sixth month the angel Gabriel was sent from God unto a city of Galilee, named Nazareth,

verses.latin_vulgate: ad virginem desponsatam viro, cui nomen erat Joseph, de domo David: et nomen virginis Maria.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πρὸς παρθένον μεμνηστευμένην ἀνδρὶ ᾧ ὄνομα ἰωσὴφ ἐξ οἴκου δαβίδ καὶ τὸ ὄνομα τῆς παρθένου μαριάμ
verses.king_james: To a virgin espoused to a man whose name was Joseph, of the house of David; and the virgin's name *was* Mary.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ingressus angelus ad eam dixit: Ave gratia plena: Dominus tecum: benedicta tu in mulieribus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἰσελθὼν ὁ ἄγγελος πρὸς αὐτὴν εἶπεν χαῖρε κεχαριτωμένη ὁ κύριος μετὰ σοῦ εὐλογημένη σὺ ἐν γυναιξίν
verses.king_james: And the angel came in unto her, and said, Hail, *thou that art* highly favoured, the Lord *is* with thee: blessed *art* thou among women.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quæ cum audisset, turbata est in sermone ejus, et cogitabat qualis esset ista salutatio.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡ δὲ ἰδοῦσα διεταράχθη ἐπὶ τῷ λόγῳ αὐτοῦ, καὶ διελογίζετο ποταπὸς εἴη ὁ ἀσπασμὸς οὗτος
verses.king_james: And when she saw *him*, she was troubled at his saying, and cast in her mind what manner of salutation this should be.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait angelus ei: Ne timeas, Maria: invenisti enim gratiam apud Deum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἶπεν ὁ ἄγγελος αὐτῇ μὴ φοβοῦ μαριάμ εὗρες γὰρ χάριν παρὰ τῷ θεῷ
verses.king_james: And the angel said unto her, Fear not, Mary: for thou hast found favour with God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce concipies in utero, et paries filium, et vocabis nomen ejus Jesum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἰδού, συλλήψῃ ἐν γαστρὶ καὶ τέξῃ υἱόν καὶ καλέσεις τὸ ὄνομα αὐτοῦ ἰησοῦν
verses.king_james: And, behold, thou shalt conceive in thy womb, and bring forth a son, and shalt call his name JESUS.

verses.latin_vulgate: hic erit magnus, et Filius Altissimi vocabitur, et dabit illi Dominus Deus sedem David patris ejus: et regnabit in domo Jacob in æternum,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὗτος ἔσται μέγας καὶ υἱὸς ὑψίστου κληθήσεται καὶ δώσει αὐτῷ κύριος ὁ θεὸς τὸν θρόνον δαβὶδ τοῦ πατρὸς αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: He shall be great, and shall be called the Son of the Highest: and the Lord God shall give unto him the throne of his father David:

verses.latin_vulgate: et regni ejus non erit finis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ βασιλεύσει ἐπὶ τὸν οἶκον ἰακὼβ εἰς τοὺς αἰῶνας καὶ τῆς βασιλείας αὐτοῦ οὐκ ἔσται τέλος
verses.king_james: And he shall reign over the house of Jacob for ever; and of his kingdom there shall be no end.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit autem Maria ad angelum: Quomodo fiet istud, quoniam virum non cognosco?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν δὲ μαριὰμ πρὸς τὸν ἄγγελον πῶς ἔσται τοῦτο ἐπεὶ ἄνδρα οὐ γινώσκω
verses.king_james: Then said Mary unto the angel, How shall this be, seeing I know not a man?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et respondens angelus dixit ei: Spiritus Sanctus superveniet in te, et virtus Altissimi obumbrabit tibi. Ideoque et quod nascetur ex te sanctum, vocabitur Filius Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀποκριθεὶς ὁ ἄγγελος εἶπεν αὐτῇ πνεῦμα ἅγιον ἐπελεύσεται ἐπὶ σέ καὶ δύναμις ὑψίστου ἐπισκιάσει σοι διὸ καὶ τὸ γεννώμενον ἅγιον κληθήσεται υἱὸς θεοῦ
verses.king_james: And the angel answered and said unto her, The Holy Ghost shall come upon thee, and the power of the Highest shall overshadow thee: therefore also that holy thing which shall be born of thee shall be called the Son of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ecce Elisabeth cognata tua, et ipsa concepit filium in senectute sua: et hic mensis sextus est illi, quæ vocatur sterilis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἰδού, ἐλισάβετ ἡ συγγενής σου καὶ αὐτὴ συνείληφυῖα υἱὸν ἐν γήρᾳ αὐτῆς καὶ οὗτος μὴν ἕκτος ἐστὶν αὐτῇ τῇ καλουμένῃ στείρᾳ
verses.king_james: And, behold, thy cousin Elisabeth, she hath also conceived a son in her old age: and this is the sixth month with her, who was called barren.

verses.latin_vulgate: quia non erit impossibile apud Deum omne verbum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτι οὐκ ἀδυνατήσει παρὰ τῷ θεῷ πᾶν ῥῆμα
verses.king_james: For with God nothing shall be impossible.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit autem Maria: Ecce ancilla Domini: fiat mihi secundum verbum tuum. Et discessit ab illa angelus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν δὲ μαριάμ ἰδού, ἡ δούλη κυρίου γένοιτό μοι κατὰ τὸ ῥῆμά σου καὶ ἀπῆλθεν ἀπ᾽ αὐτῆς ὁ ἄγγελος
verses.king_james: And Mary said, Behold the handmaid of the Lord; be it unto me according to thy word. And the angel departed from her.

verses.latin_vulgate: Exsurgens autem Maria in diebus illis, abiit in montana cum festinatione, in civitatem Juda:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀναστᾶσα δὲ μαριὰμ ἐν ταῖς ἡμέραις ταύταις ἐπορεύθη εἰς τὴν ὀρεινὴν μετὰ σπουδῆς εἰς πόλιν ἰούδα
verses.king_james: And Mary arose in those days, and went into the hill country with haste, into a city of Juda;

verses.latin_vulgate: et intravit in domum Zachariæ, et salutavit Elisabeth.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἰσῆλθεν εἰς τὸν οἶκον ξαχαρίου καὶ ἠσπάσατο τὴν ἐλισάβετ
verses.king_james: And entered into the house of Zacharias, and saluted Elisabeth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et factum est, ut audivit salutationem Mariæ Elisabeth, exsultavit infans in utero ejus: et repleta est Spiritu Sancto Elisabeth:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐγένετο ὡς ἤκουσεν ἡ ἐλισάβετ τὸν ἀσπασμὸν τῆς μαρίας ἐσκίρτησεν τὸ βρέφος ἐν τῇ κοιλίᾳ αὐτῆς καὶ ἐπλήσθη πνεύματος ἁγίου ἡ ἐλισάβετ
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, that, when Elisabeth heard the salutation of Mary, the babe leaped in her womb; and Elisabeth was filled with the Holy Ghost:

verses.latin_vulgate: et exclamavit voce magna, et dixit: Benedicta tu inter mulieres, et benedictus fructus ventris tui.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀνεφώνησεν φωνῇ μεγάλῃ καὶ εἶπεν εὐλογημένη σὺ ἐν γυναιξίν καὶ εὐλογημένος ὁ καρπὸς τῆς κοιλίας σου
verses.king_james: And she spake out with a loud voice, and said, Blessed *art* thou among women, and blessed *is* the fruit of thy womb.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et unde hoc mihi, ut veniat mater Domini mei ad me?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πόθεν μοι τοῦτο ἵνα ἔλθῃ ἡ μήτηρ τοῦ κυρίου μου πρὸς μέ
verses.king_james: And whence *is* this to me, that the mother of my Lord should come to me?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce enim ut facta est vox salutationis tuæ in auribus meis, exsultavit in gaudio infans in utero meo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἰδού, γὰρ ὡς ἐγένετο ἡ φωνὴ τοῦ ἀσπασμοῦ σου εἰς τὰ ὦτά μου ἐσκίρτησεν ἐν ἀγαλλιάσει τὸ βρέφος ἐν τῇ κοιλίᾳ μου
verses.king_james: For, lo, as soon as the voice of thy salutation sounded in mine ears, the babe leaped in my womb for joy.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et beata, quæ credidisti, quoniam perficientur ea, quæ dicta sunt tibi a Domino.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ μακαρία ἡ πιστεύσασα ὅτι ἔσται τελείωσις τοῖς λελαλημένοις αὐτῇ παρὰ κυρίου
verses.king_james: And blessed *is* she that believed: for there shall be a performance of those things which were told her from the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait Maria: [Magnificat anima mea Dominum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἶπεν μαριάμ μεγαλύνει ἥ ψυχή μου τόν κύριόν
verses.king_james: And Mary said, My soul doth magnify the Lord,

verses.latin_vulgate: et exsultavit spiritus meus in Deo salutari meo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἠγαλλίασεν τὸ πνεῦμά μου ἐπὶ τῷ θεῷ τῷ σωτῆρί μου
verses.king_james: And my spirit hath rejoiced in God my Saviour.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia respexit humilitatem ancillæ suæ: ecce enim ex hoc beatam me dicent omnes generationes,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτι ἐπέβλεψεν ἐπὶ τὴν ταπείνωσιν τῆς δούλης αὐτοῦ ἰδού, γὰρ ἀπὸ τοῦ νῦν μακαριοῦσίν με πᾶσαι αἱ γενεαί
verses.king_james: For he hath regarded the low estate of his handmaiden: for, behold, from henceforth all generations shall call me blessed.

verses.latin_vulgate: quia fecit mihi magna qui potens est: et sanctum nomen ejus,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτι ἐποίησέν μοι μεγάλεῖα ὁ δυνατός καὶ ἅγιον τὸ ὄνομα αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: For he that is mighty hath done to me great things; and holy *is* his name.

verses.latin_vulgate: et misericordia ejus a progenie in progenies timentibus eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ τὸ ἔλεος αὐτοῦ εἰς γενεὰς γενεῶν τοῖς φοβουμένοις αὐτόν
verses.king_james: And his mercy *is* on them that fear him from generation to generation.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fecit potentiam in brachio suo: dispersit superbos mente cordis sui.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐποίησεν κράτος ἐν βραχίονι αὐτοῦ διεσκόρπισεν ὑπερηφάνους διανοίᾳ καρδίας αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: He hath shewed strength with his arm; he hath scattered the proud in the imagination of their hearts.

verses.latin_vulgate: Deposuit potentes de sede, et exaltavit humiles.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καθεῖλεν δυνάστας ἀπὸ θρόνων καὶ ὕψωσεν ταπεινούς
verses.king_james: He hath put down the mighty from *their* seats, and exalted them of low degree.

verses.latin_vulgate: Esurientes implevit bonis: et divites dimisit inanes.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πεινῶντας ἐνέπλησεν ἀγαθῶν καὶ πλουτοῦντας ἐξαπέστειλεν κενούς
verses.king_james: He hath filled the hungry with good things; and the rich he hath sent empty away.

verses.latin_vulgate: Suscepit Israël puerum suum, recordatus misericordiæ suæ:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀντελάβετο ἰσραὴλ παιδὸς αὐτοῦ μνησθῆναι ἐλέους
verses.king_james: He hath holpen his servant Israel, in remembrance of *his* mercy;

verses.latin_vulgate: sicut locutus est ad patres nostros, Abraham et semini ejus in sæcula.]
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καθὼς ἐλάλησεν πρὸς τοὺς πατέρας ἡμῶν τῷ ἀβραὰμ καὶ τῷ σπέρματι αὐτοῦ εἰς τὸν αἰῶνα
verses.king_james: As he spake to our fathers, to Abraham, and to his seed for ever.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mansit autem Maria cum illa quasi mensibus tribus: et reversa est in domum suam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἕμεινεν δὲ μαριὰμ σὺν αὐτῇ ὡσεὶ μῆνας τρεῖς καὶ ὑπέστρεψεν εἰς τὸν οἶκον αὐτῆς
verses.king_james: And Mary abode with her about three months, and returned to her own house.

verses.latin_vulgate: Elisabeth autem impletum est tempus pariendi, et peperit filium.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τῇ δὲ ἐλισάβετ ἐπλήσθη ὁ χρόνος τοῦ τεκεῖν αὐτήν καὶ ἐγέννησεν υἱόν
verses.king_james: Now Elisabeth's full time came that she should be delivered; and she brought forth a son.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et audierunt vicini et cognati ejus quia magnificavit Dominus misericordiam suam cum illa, et congratulabantur ei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἤκουσαν οἱ περίοικοι καὶ οἱ συγγενεῖς αὐτῆς ὅτι ἐμεγάλυνεν κύριος τὸ ἔλεος αὐτοῦ μετ᾽ αὐτῆς καὶ συνέχαιρον αὐτῇ
verses.king_james: And her neighbours and her cousins heard how the Lord had shewed great mercy upon her; and they rejoiced with her.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et factum est in die octavo, venerunt circumcidere puerum, et vocabant eum nomine patris sui Zachariam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐγένετο ἐν τῇ ὀγδόῃ ἡμέρᾳ ἦλθον περιτεμεῖν τὸ παιδίον καὶ ἐκάλουν αὐτὸ ἐπὶ τῷ ὀνόματι τοῦ πατρὸς αὐτοῦ ξαχαρίαν
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, that on the eighth day they came to circumcise the child; and they called him Zacharias, after the name of his father.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et respondens mater ejus, dixit: Nequaquam, sed vocabitur Joannes.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀποκριθεῖσα ἡ μήτηρ αὐτοῦ εἶπεν οὐχί ἀλλὰ κληθήσεται ἰωάννης
verses.king_james: And his mother answered and said, Not *so* ; but he shall be called John.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixerunt ad illam: Quia nemo est in cognatione tua, qui vocetur hoc nomine.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἶπον πρὸς αὐτὴν ὅτι οὐδείς ἐστιν ἐν τῇ συγγενεία σου ὃς καλεῖται τῷ ὀνόματι τούτῳ
verses.king_james: And they said unto her, There is none of thy kindred that is called by this name.

verses.latin_vulgate: Innuebant autem patri ejus, quem vellet vocari eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐνένευον δὲ τῷ πατρὶ αὐτοῦ τὸ τί ἂν θέλοι καλεῖσθαι αὐτόν
verses.king_james: And they made signs to his father, how he would have him called.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et postulans pugillarem scripsit, dicens: Joannes est nomen ejus. Et mirati sunt universi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ αἰτήσας πινακίδιον ἔγραψεν λέγων, ἰωάννης ἐστὶν τὸ ὄνομα αὐτοῦ καὶ ἐθαύμασαν πάντες
verses.king_james: And he asked for a writing table, and wrote, saying, His name is John. And they marvelled all.

verses.latin_vulgate: Apertum est autem illico os ejus, et lingua ejus, et loquebatur benedicens Deum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀνεῴχθη δὲ τὸ στόμα αὐτοῦ παραχρῆμα καὶ ἡ γλῶσσα αὐτοῦ καὶ ἐλάλει εὐλογῶν τὸν θεόν
verses.king_james: And his mouth was opened immediately, and his tongue *loosed*, and he spake, and praised God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et factus est timor super omnes vicinos eorum: et super omnia montana Judææ divulgabantur omnia verba hæc:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐγένετο ἐπὶ πάντας φόβος τοὺς περιοικοῦντας αὐτούς καὶ ἐν ὅλῃ τῇ ὀρεινῇ τῆς ἰουδαίας διελαλεῖτο πάντα τὰ ῥήματα ταῦτα
verses.king_james: And fear came on all that dwelt round about them: and all these sayings were noised abroad throughout all the hill country of Judaea.

verses.latin_vulgate: et posuerunt omnes qui audierant in corde suo, dicentes: Quis, putas, puer iste erit? etenim manus Domini erat cum illo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἔθεντο πάντες οἱ ἀκούσαντες ἐν τῇ καρδίᾳ αὐτῶν λέγοντες τί ἄρα τὸ παιδίον τοῦτο ἔσται καὶ χεὶρ κυρίου ἦν μετ᾽ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And all they that heard *them* laid *them* up in their hearts, saying, What manner of child shall this be! And the hand of the Lord was with him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et Zacharias pater ejus repletus est Spiritu Sancto: et prophetavit, dicens:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ξαχαρίας ὁ πατὴρ αὐτοῦ ἐπλήσθη πνεύματος ἁγίου καὶ προεφήτευσεν λέγων
verses.king_james: And his father Zacharias was filled with the Holy Ghost, and prophesied, saying,

verses.latin_vulgate: [Benedictus Dominus Deus Israël, quia visitavit, et fecit redemptionem plebis suæ:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εὐλογητὸς κύριος ὁ θεὸς τοῦ ἰσραήλ ὅτι ἐπεσκέψατο καὶ ἐποίησεν λύτρωσιν τῷ λαῷ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: Blessed *be* the Lord God of Israel; for he hath visited and redeemed his people,

verses.latin_vulgate: et erexit cornu salutis nobis in domo David pueri sui,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἤγειρεν κέρας σωτηρίας ἡμῖν ἐν τῷ οἴκῳ δαβὶδ τοῦ παιδὸς αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And hath raised up an horn of salvation for us in the house of his servant David;

verses.latin_vulgate: sicut locutum est per os sanctorum, qui a sæculo sunt, prophetarum ejus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καθὼς ἐλάλησεν διὰ στόματος τῶν ἁγίων τῶν ἀπ᾽ αἰῶνος προφητῶν αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: As he spake by the mouth of his holy prophets, which have been since the world began:

verses.latin_vulgate: salutem ex inimicis nostris, et de manu omnium qui oderunt nos:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: σωτηρίαν ἐξ ἐχθρῶν ἡμῶν καὶ ἐκ χειρὸς πάντων τῶν μισούντων ἡμᾶς
verses.king_james: That we should be saved from our enemies, and from the hand of all that hate us;

verses.latin_vulgate: ad faciendam misericordiam cum patribus nostris: et memorari testamenti sui sancti:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ποιῆσαι ἔλεος μετὰ τῶν πατέρων ἡμῶν καὶ μνησθῆναι διαθήκης ἁγίας αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: To perform the mercy *promised* to our fathers, and to remember his holy covenant;

verses.latin_vulgate: jusjurandum, quod juravit ad Abraham patrem nostrum, daturum se nobis
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅρκον ὃν ὤμοσεν πρὸς ἀβραὰμ τὸν πατέρα ἡμῶν
verses.king_james: The oath which he sware to our father Abraham,

verses.latin_vulgate: ut sine timore, de manu inimicorum nostrorum liberati, serviamus illi
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦ δοῦναί ἡμῖν ἀφόβως ἐκ χειρὸς τῶν ἐχθρῶν ἡμῶν ῥυσθέντας λατρεύειν αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: That he would grant unto us, that we being delivered out of the hand of our enemies might serve him without fear,

verses.latin_vulgate: in sanctitate et justitia coram ipso, omnibus diebus nostris.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν ὁσιότητι καὶ δικαιοσύνῃ ἐνώπιον αὐτοῦ πάσας τὰς ἡμέρας τὴς ζωῆς ἡμῶν
verses.king_james: In holiness and righteousness before him, all the days of our life.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et tu puer, propheta Altissimi vocaberis: præibis enim ante faciem Domini parare vias ejus,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ σὺ παιδίον προφήτης ὑψίστου κληθήσῃ προπορεύσῃ γὰρ πρὸ προσώπου κυρίου ἑτοιμάσαι ὁδοὺς αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And thou, child, shalt be called the prophet of the Highest: for thou shalt go before the face of the Lord to prepare his ways;

verses.latin_vulgate: ad dandam scientiam salutis plebi ejus in remissionem peccatorum eorum
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦ δοῦναι γνῶσιν σωτηρίας τῷ λαῷ αὐτοῦ ἐν ἀφέσει ἁμαρτιῶν αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: To give knowledge of salvation unto his people by the remission of their sins,

verses.latin_vulgate: per viscera misericordiæ Dei nostri, in quibus visitavit nos, oriens ex alto:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὰ σπλάγχνα ἐλέους θεοῦ ἡμῶν ἐν οἷς ἐπεσκέψατο ἡμᾶς ἀνατολὴ ἐξ ὕψους
verses.king_james: Through the tender mercy of our God; whereby the dayspring from on high hath visited us,

verses.latin_vulgate: illuminare his qui in tenebris et in umbra mortis sedent: ad dirigendos pedes nostros in viam pacis.]
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐπιφᾶναι τοῖς ἐν σκότει καὶ σκιᾷ θανάτου καθημένοις τοῦ κατευθῦναι τοὺς πόδας ἡμῶν εἰς ὁδὸν εἰρήνης
verses.king_james: To give light to them that sit in darkness and *in* the shadow of death, to guide our feet into the way of peace.

verses.latin_vulgate: Puer autem crescebat, et confortabatur spiritu: et erat in desertis usque in diem ostensionis suæ ad Israël.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὸ δὲ παιδίον ηὔξανεν καὶ ἐκραταιοῦτο πνεύματι καὶ ἦν ἐν ταῖς ἐρήμοις ἕως ἡμέρας ἀναδείξεως αὐτοῦ πρὸς τὸν ἰσραήλ
verses.king_james: And the child grew, and waxed strong in spirit, and was in the deserts till the day of his shewing unto Israel.
